# =============================================================================
# SmartEVSE-3 v3 — main application component
#
# Pulls in the existing src/ tree as a single component. Excludes:
#   - src/mongoose.c            (own component: components/mongoose)
#   - src/packed_fs.c           (generated by components/web_assets)
#   - src/evse.c                (CH32 firmware, not built for ESP32 target)
#   - src/ch32.cpp              (CH32-only code)
#   - src/utils.cpp             (CH32 helpers; harmless to keep but excluded
#                                 to keep the v3 ESP32 build clean)
# =============================================================================

idf_component_register(
    SRC_DIRS
        "${CMAKE_CURRENT_LIST_DIR}/../../src"
        "${CMAKE_CURRENT_LIST_DIR}"
    INCLUDE_DIRS
        "${CMAKE_CURRENT_LIST_DIR}/../../src"
    EXCLUDE_SRCS
        "mongoose.c"
        "evse.c"
        "ch32.cpp"
        "utils.cpp"
        "modbus.cpp"   # ESP32 builds use the ModbusServer/Client code in
                       # esp32.cpp; this .cpp is a CH32-only alternative.
        "meter.cpp"    # same reason as modbus.cpp
        # packed_fs.c is included — it is generated by
        # components/web_assets/CMakeLists.txt at configure time
        # and provides mg_unpack/mg_unlist which mongoose 7.20
        # references when MG_ENABLE_PACKED_FS=1.
    REQUIRES
        arduino_compat
        arduinojson
        mongoose
        emodbus
        micro_ocpp
        micro_ocpp_mongoose
        esp_wifi
        esp_netif
        esp_event
        nvs_flash
        esp_http_client
        esp_https_ota
        app_update      # esp_ota_* moved to app_update in IDF v5+
        esp_partition
        esp_adc         # esp_adc_cal was renamed to esp_adc in v6
        driver
        esp_lcd
        esp_timer
        spi_flash
        esp_eth       # esp_eth.h / esp_eth_mac.h / esp_eth_phy.h (CH390 driver)
        onewire_bus  # vendored espressif/onewire_bus (RMT backend) with
                     # SmartEVSE timing fix; see components/onewire_bus
        esp_driver_mcpwm   # mcpwm_prelude.h — MCPWM CP PWM + comparator-triggered ADC sampling
        efuse         # esp_efuse.h / EFUSE_BLK1/3 / esp_efuse_read_block
        mbedtls       # mbedtls_pk_parse_public_key / mbedtls_pk_verify_ext (OTA sig verify)
)

# These compile flags must propagate from the original platformio.ini
# `[common].build_flags` and are consumed by Mongoose / MicroOcpp headers.
# Note: the MO_* and MG_* flags are owned by the micro_ocpp / mongoose
# components respectively to avoid `-Werror=redefined` from CMake
# inheriting PUBLIC definitions into component compile commands.
target_compile_options(${COMPONENT_LIB} PRIVATE
    -Wno-stringop-truncation
)
target_compile_definitions(${COMPONENT_LIB} PRIVATE
    ENABLE_OCPP=1
    # DBG sets the output of debug
    # 0=silent, 1=telnet or 2=USB-serial debug
    DBG=2
    # LOG_LEVEL drives the _LOG_* macros in src/debug.h when DBG != 0
    # 0 = silent, 5 = verbose.
    LOG_LEVEL=3
    SMARTEVSE_VERSION=30
    # Define CH32=0 to disable the CH32V003 firmware path in
    # evse.c / OneWire.cpp / ch32.cpp. With SMARTEVSE_VERSION set,
    # the #ifndef SMARTEVSE_VERSION guards in those files fall
    # through to the ESP32 stub branches.
    CH32=0
    # ESP32=1 is the same switch eModbus uses to pick the ESP32 branch
    # of its options.h. The app includes <ModbusServerRTU.h> from the
    # eModbus component, so its TUs also need the matching define.
    ESP32=1
    # The eModbus options.h also checks ARDUINO via the legacy Arduino
    # core define. We keep the define at 200 (matching eModbus 1.7.x's
    # expected "modern" Arduino core) so any ARDUINO-version branches
    # take the v2+ path. The actual Arduino surface comes from the
    # arduino_compat shim, not the real Arduino core.
    ARDUINO=200
)
